original_image_tags: A yellowed, aged document page. The main typed text block is printed in reverse (mirrored), indicating this is a carbon copy or transfer sheet. At the top (which is the bottom of the reversed header), faint reversed text reads "UNITED STATES GOVERNMENT" and "OFFICE MEMORANDUM". A standard FBI routing header is partially visible but reversed. The document contains two holes punched at the top and a strip of brown tape on the upper right edge. A large blue stamp reading "RECEIVED U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE FBI RECD. ESPIONAGE AUG 4 5 25 PM '49" is oriented correctly at the bottom of the page. The main body text is a typed paragraph.

elements.description: Multiple visible stamps and markings. At the bottom left, an upside-down stamped block reads 'U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE', 'FBI', 'RECD. ESPIONAGE', 'AUG 4 5 25 PM '49', 'RECEIVED'. At the top right, brown tape and a stamp partially reading 'Office' and 'Mail' are visible. Faint, illegible typed text is visible from the reverse side of the paper.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the core visual content: a large blue 'RECEIVED U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE FBI RECD. ESPIONAGE' stamp, brown tape on the upper right, and faint reversed/legible text from the reverse side, but they differ on the exact quoted stamp text and the presence/absence of other markings.
